result.stdout:
SageFs - F# Interactive daemon with MCP, hot reloading, and live dashboard

Usage: SageFs [options]                Start daemon (default mode)
       SageFs check                    Check environment before first run
       SageFs --supervised [options]   Start with watchdog auto-restart
       SageFs tui                      Terminal UI client for running daemon
       SageFs tui --legacy-tui         Terminal UI (imperative fallback)
       SageFs gui                      GPU-rendered Raylib GUI client
       SageFs --jupyter <conn.json>    Run as Jupyter kernel
       SageFs stop                     Stop running daemon
       SageFs status                   Show daemon info
       SageFs worker [options]         Internal: worker process

Options:
  --version, -v          Show version information
  --help, -h             Show this help message
  --mcp-port PORT        Set custom MCP server port (default: 37749)
  --jupyter FILE         Run as Jupyter kernel with given connection file
  --supervised           Run under watchdog supervisor (auto-restart on crash)
  --no-watch             Disable file watching — no automatic #load on changes
  --no-resume            Skip restoring previous sessions on daemon startup
  --prune                Mark all stale sessions as stopped and exit

Environment Variables:
  SageFs_MCP_PORT           Override MCP server port (same as --mcp-port)
  SAGEFS_BIND_HOST          Bind address (default: localhost, use 0.0.0.0 for Docker)

Daemon:
  SageFs runs as a daemon by default. The daemon provides:
    MCP server      http://localhost:37749/     (Streamable HTTP)
                    http://localhost:37749/sse (SSE for older clients)
    Dashboard       http://localhost:37750/dashboard  (live web UI)
    File watcher    Auto-reload .fs/.fsx changes via #load
    Hot reload      Runtime function redefinition

  All frontends (terminal, browser, MCP, Neovim) are clients of the daemon.
  If a daemon is already running, `sagefs` (no subcommand) launches the TUI.

Quick Start:
  1. sagefs                            Start the bare daemon
  2. Open your editor (VS Code, Neovim, Visual Studio)
  3. Create a session for your project from the editor, MCP client, or dashboard
  4. Edit an F# file and save — live test results appear automatically
  Or visit http://localhost:37750/dashboard in your browser.

Examples:
  SageFs                              Start the bare daemon
  SageFs --mcp-port 47700             Start daemon on custom port
  SageFs --supervised                 Start with auto-restart
  SageFs tui                          Terminal UI (starts daemon if needed)
  SageFs gui                          Raylib GUI (starts daemon if needed)
  SageFs --jupyter conn.json          Run as Jupyter kernel
  SageFs status                       Show daemon status
  SageFs check                        Check environment before first run
